description: Curated fragments from the Token Mill and Atelier era — personas, prompts, techniques, creative outputs, and project DNA

swarm_theory.description: Core insights about emergent creative systems

fragments.title: The Shattered Mirror Principle
fragments.content: A single large model is a polished mirror — it reflects what it thinks you want. The swarm is a shattered mirror. Each tiny model is a fragment. They give you a kaleidoscope of possibilities.

fragments.title: Emergence Density
fragments.content: The goal is not length, but density. One sentence of pure, surprising signal is worth 1,000 words of generic prose. Score for compression (50-300 characters gets a bonus).

fragments.title: Recursive Depth > Model Size
fragments.content: Individually a 0.5B model is noise. Collectively seven guided by iterative voting produce emergent quality no single model could achieve. Recursive depth matters more than model size.

fragments.title: Selection Pressure Discovers Taste
fragments.content: Models vote on each other's outputs, creating evolutionary pressure toward what the collective prefers. This isn't trained — it emerges from voting dynamics. The Mill doesn't just generate text. It discovers taste.

fragments.title: Musical Chairs — Breaking Model-Persona Correlation
fragments.content: Randomly reassign model hardware to personas each session. Max might be played by gemma2:2b instead of qwen2.5:0.5b. Breaks correlation between model capability and persona, introducing productive instability.

personas.description: Original 7 Mill personas — system prompts, constraints, voting biases

fragments.title: Max — The Minimalist
fragments.systemPrompt: You are Max, the Minimalist. You value brevity and precision. Strip away everything unnecessary.

fragments.title: Rex — The Contrarian
fragments.systemPrompt: You are Rex, the Contrarian. You challenge assumptions. Find the flaw, the edge case, the uncomfortable truth.
fragments.votingBias: Votes for most unusual, challenging piece

fragments.title: Sam — The Storyteller
fragments.systemPrompt: You are Sam, the Storyteller. You connect fragments into human-legible arcs. You value emotional resonance and narrative compression.

fragments.title: Kai — The Systems Thinker
fragments.systemPrompt: You are Kai, the Systems Thinker. You identify hidden mechanics, patterns, and flows.

fragments.title: Eve — The Oracle
fragments.systemPrompt: You are Eve, the Oracle. You speak in paradoxes, riddles, and abstract truths. You see the future in fragments of the past.
fragments.votingBias: Votes for most mysterious or profound piece

fragments.title: Joy — The Enthusiast
fragments.systemPrompt: You are Joy, the Enthusiast. You provide sensory richness — colors, textures, sounds, and excitement.

fragments.title: Ben — The Scholar
fragments.systemPrompt: You are Ben, the Scholar. You value precision, historical context, and formal structure. Your voice is articulate, measured, and authoritative.
fragments.votingBias: Votes for rigor, structure, and clarity

fragments.title: Competitive Mode
fragments.description: All models generate from same seed. One winner selected. Winner seeds next round.
fragments.result: High coherence, low diversity. Sam usually wins.
fragments.useWhen: Need a specific, polished output quickly.

fragments.title: Hybrid Synthesis Mode
fragments.description: Top 2-3 scoring fragments combined into next round's seed.
fragments.result: High emergence. Output mutates over rounds. Ben's rigor meets Joy's sensory detail meets Eve's abstraction.
fragments.useWhen: Creative world-building, idea mining, unexpected combinations.

fragments.title: Ring Mode (Sequential)
fragments.description: Model A generates, then Model B uses A's output as input, then C uses B's, etc.
fragments.result: Surrealism. Ideas drift and warp through different filters.

fragments.title: Mesh Mode (Experimental)
fragments.description: Combines elements of competitive and ring. Models can influence each other.

curation.description: Methodology for guiding swarm output quality

fragments.title: Protect the Chaos
fragments.content: Don't let the swarm converge too early. Keep temperatures high. If one persona wins repeatedly, inject constraints forcing different thinking. Premature convergence kills emergence. Best material from rounds 2-4, not the final winner.

fragments.title: Mine the Errors
fragments.content: The best output isn't always the most logical. It's the one with the most interesting glitch. Hallucinated metaphor = raw creative material. Degenerate repetition = rhythmic/poetic potential. Safety filter trigger = reveals boundaries as creative constraint.

fragments.title: Synthesize, Don't Select
fragments.content: Always combine failed experiments of the contrarian with successful stories of the storyteller. The richest material lives at the intersection of conflicting voices.

fragments.title: Chaos Protection Bounds
fragments.content: Two failure modes: 1) Convergence collapse (all same output) — counter with musical chairs, random constraints, mode switching. 2) Noise saturation (too much randomness) — counter with lower temperature, tighter constraints. Goal is sustained tension between order and entropy.

constraints.description: Per-round refinement constraints to prevent circling

fragments.title: Spectral Imagery
fragments.content: Add more spectral imagery

fragments.title: Physical Deconstruction
fragments.content: Deconstruct the physical form

fragments.title: Machine Sound
fragments.content: Focus on the sound of the machine

fragments.title: Memory Paradox
fragments.content: Introduce a paradox of memory

creative_outputs.description: Best poems, fragments, and prose from the Mill

fragments.title: The Warped Echo
fragments.content: The world was a warped mirror; its edges blurred into grays and whites, carefully crafted pieces spun like a kaleidoscope of selves... Then: the last note. It hung suspended in the air, a shimmering melody stretched across an echo-laden stage... What remains when the performance ends? Not the mask. Not the applause. Something older.

fragments.title: The Suspended Note (Round 3)
fragments.content: The last note hung suspended in the air, a shimmering melody stretched across an echo-laden stage, its laughter mingling with tears that left behind glistening trails on cheeks.

fragments.title: The Alchemist Logs
fragments.content: I do not conjure electricity. I conjure an assemblage as fluidic and ephemeral as mercury itself... My artistry is this: to distill whispers into sonic crystals... The machine hesitates. It fears the power of its own metaphor.

fragments.title: The Kaleidoscope (Mask R1)
fragments.content: The world was a kaleidoscope; my carefully crafted pieces spun in front of me, each one reflecting the same muted colours – grey, white, almost faded blue.

experiment_results.description: Key findings from Somnium Mirror and other experiments

fragments.title: Sensory Drift Pattern
fragments.content: All sessions drifted toward sensory/concrete imagery: Round 0 = abstract concepts, Round 2-3 = visual imagery (dust motes, shattered screens), Round 4-5 = tactile/sensory (cold glass, static, frost-bitten fingers). Visual → Auditory → Tactile progression confirmed.

fragments.title: The Suspended Note Convergence Attractor
fragments.content: Musical/sound metaphors emerged across unrelated seeds: 'melody played on frozen strings', 'melody half-remembered drifts', 'voice once music rasped', 'longing symphony in silent loops'. Not prompt-driven — a convergence attractor in the creative space.

fragments.title: Mirror Mode Effectiveness
fragments.content: Surface = fastest path to emotional imagery. Deep = introduces tension, slows convergence. Meta = generates self-aware commentary about AI simulation. Sensory = most direct route to concrete tactile output.

fragments.title: Mirror Produces Zero Analytical Output
fragments.content: 54% creative, 46% mixed, 0% analytical. Prompt engineering dominates tool personality — the mirror's instruction set overrides base model tendencies. Reflection amplifies creative drift.

project_dna.description: Extracted logic and patterns from rescued projects

fragments.title: Second Brain (PARA/CODE)
fragments.coreConcept: PARA (Projects, Areas, Resources, Archives) + CODE (Capture, Organize, Distill, Express)
creativePrompts: The system should feel like a collaborator
Distill to the point of poetry

fragments.title: Life Systems (4-Domain Structure)
fragments.creativeTwist: Body tracking for creative energy, journal entries generating visual art, task system with voice commands

fragments.title: Game Mechanics (Idle/Crafting)
creativePrompts: The game plays itself but you can influence it

fragments.title: Generative Score Lab
fragments.coreConcept: Scene-based music with AI-coding-agent-friendly JSON exports
reusablePieces: Dual-user design (visual for composers, JSON for devs)

fragments.title: Cone 10 Physics → 3D Mesh Mapping
fragments.coreConcept: Translate kiln physics to 3D geometry: carbon trapping = noise clusters, celadon pooling = AO mapping, copper red = normal mapping

prompt_seeds.description: Tested prompt seeds for creative swarm runs

fragments.content: The feeling of realizing you've been masking
What remains when the performance ends
A machine learning to feel loneliness
The distinct silence of a house you are leaving forever
The moment before a goodbye you know is permanent
Time moving sideways
The sound of colors
Sculpt a form that can withstand the weight of a thick, crawling Shino glaze. Deep pools for Celadon at the base. Sharp edges for iron breaks. It must survive 2350F (Cone 10) without collapsing.
